verses.latin_vulgate: Adam vero cognovit uxorem suam Hevam, quæ concepit et peperit Cain, dicens : Possedi hominem per Deum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָ֣אָדָ֔ם יָדַ֖ע אֶת־חַוָּ֣ה אִשְׁתּ֑וֹ וַתַּ֙הַר֙ וַתֵּ֣לֶד אֶת־קַ֔יִן וַתֹּ֕אמֶר קָנִ֥יתִי אִ֖ישׁ אֶת־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Adam knew Eve his wife; who conceived and brought forth Cain, saying: I have gotten a man through God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Adam tunsi emäntänsä Hevan, ja hän tuli raskaaksi, ja synnytti Kainin, ja sanoi: minulla on mies Herra.
verses.king_james: And Adam knew Eve his wife; and she conceived, and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a man from the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rursumque peperit fratrem ejus Abel. Fuit autem Abel pastor ovium, et Cain agricola.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣סֶף לָלֶ֔דֶת אֶת־אָחִ֖יו אֶת־הָ֑בֶל וַֽיְהִי־הֶ֙בֶל֙ רֹ֣עֵה צֹ֔אן וְקַ֕יִן הָיָ֖ה עֹבֵ֥ד אֲדָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And again she brought forth his brother Abel. And Abel was a shepherd, and Cain a husbandman.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja taas hän synnytti hänen veljensä Habelin. Ja Habel tuli lampuriksi , mutta Kain peltomieheksi.
verses.king_james: And she again bare his brother Abel. And Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem post multos dies ut offerret Cain de fructibus terræ munera Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִ֖י מִקֵּ֣ץ יָמִ֑ים וַיָּבֵ֨א קַ֜יִן מִפְּרִ֧י הָֽאֲדָמָ֛ה מִנְחָ֖ה לַֽיהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass after many days, that Cain offered, of the fruits of the earth, gifts to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui muutamain päiväin perästä, että Kain uhrasi Herralle lahjan maan hedelmistä.
verses.king_james: And in process of time it came to pass, that Cain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abel quoque obtulit de primogenitis gregis sui, et de adipibus eorum : et respexit Dominus ad Abel, et ad munera ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֶ֨בֶל הֵבִ֥יא גַם־ה֛וּא מִבְּכֹר֥וֹת צֹאנ֖וֹ וּמֵֽחֶלְבֵהֶ֑ן וַיִּ֣שַׁע יְהֹוָ֔ה אֶל־הֶ֖בֶל וְאֶל־מִנְחָתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Abel also offered of the firstlings of his flock, and of their fat: and the Lord had respect to Abel, and to his offerings.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Habel myös uhrasi laumansa esikoisista, ja heidän lihavuudestansa. Ja Herra katsoi (leppyisesti) Habelin ja hänen uhrinsa puoleen.
verses.king_james: And Abel, he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and of the fat thereof. And the Lord had respect unto Abel and to his offering:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad Cain vero, et ad munera illius non respexit : iratusque est Cain vehementer, et concidit vultus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶל־קַ֥יִן וְאֶל־מִנְחָת֖וֹ לֹ֣א שָׁעָ֑ה וַיִּ֤חַר לְקַ֙יִן֙ מְאֹ֔ד וַֽיִּפְּל֖וּ פָּנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: But to Cain and his offerings he had no respect: and Cain was exceeding angry, and his countenance fell.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Kainin ja hänen uhrinsa puoleen ei hän (leppyisesti) katsonut: silloin Kain vihastui sangen kovin, ja hänen hahmonsa muuttui.
verses.king_james: But unto Cain and to his offering he had not respect. And Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Dominus ad eum : Quare iratus es ? et cur concidit facies tua ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־קָ֑יִן לָ֚מָּה חָ֣רָה לָ֔ךְ וְלָ֖מָּה נָפְל֥וּ פָנֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to him: Why art thou angry? and why is thy countenance fallen?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Herra sanoi Kainille: miksis olet vihainen? Ja miksi hahmos muuttuu?
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Cain, Why art thou wroth? and why is thy countenance fallen?

verses.latin_vulgate: nonne si bene egeris, recipies : sin autem male, statim in foribus peccatum aderit ? sed sub te erit appetitus ejus, et tu dominaberis illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲל֤וֹא אִם־תֵּיטִיב֙ שְׂאֵ֔ת וְאִם֙ לֹ֣א תֵיטִ֔יב לַפֶּ֖תַח חַטָּ֣את רֹבֵ֑ץ וְאֵלֶ֙יךָ֙ תְּשׁ֣וּקָת֔וֹ וְאַתָּ֖ה תִּמְשׇׁל־בּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: If thou do well, shalt thou not receive? but if ill, shall not sin forthwith be present at the door? but the lust thereof shall be under thee, and thou shalt have dominion over it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö se niin ole? jos hyvin teet niin syntis anteeksi annetaan; ja jolles hyvin tee, niin synti väijyy oven edessä, ja hänen himonsa on sinun tykös, vaan hallitse sinä häntä.
verses.king_james: If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sin lieth at the door. And unto thee shall be his desire, and thou shalt rule over him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Cain ad Abel fratrem suum : Egrediamur foras. Cumque essent in agro, consurrexit Cain adversus fratrem suum Abel, et interfecit eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר קַ֖יִן אֶל־הֶ֣בֶל אָחִ֑יו וַֽיְהִי֙ בִּהְיוֹתָ֣ם בַּשָּׂדֶ֔ה וַיָּ֥קׇם קַ֛יִן אֶל־הֶ֥בֶל אָחִ֖יו וַיַּהַרְגֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Cain said to Abel his brother: Let us go forth abroad. And when they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel, and slew him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Kain puhui veljensä Habelin kanssa. Ja tapahtui heidän kedolla ollessansa, että Kain karkasi veljensä Habelin päälle, ja tappoi hänen.
verses.king_james: And Cain talked with Abel his brother: and it came to pass, when they were in the field, that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Dominus ad Cain : Ubi est Abel frater tuus ? Qui respondit : Nescio : num custos fratris mei sum ego ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יְהֹוָה֙ אֶל־קַ֔יִן אֵ֖י הֶ֣בֶל אָחִ֑יךָ וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ לֹ֣א יָדַ֔עְתִּי הֲשֹׁמֵ֥ר אָחִ֖י אָנֹֽכִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to Cain: Where is thy brother Abel? And he answered: I know not: am I my brother's keeper?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Herra sanoi Kainille: kussa on Habel sinun veljes? hän vastasi: en minä tiedä: olenko minä veljeni vartia?
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto Cain, Where is Abel thy brother? And he said, I know not: Am I my brother’s keeper?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ad eum : Quid fecisti ? vox sanguinis fratris tui clamat ad me de terra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר מֶ֣ה עָשִׂ֑יתָ ק֚וֹל דְּמֵ֣י אָחִ֔יךָ צֹעֲקִ֥ים אֵלַ֖י מִן־הָֽאֲדָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to him: What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood crieth to me from the earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: mitäs tehnyt olet? veljes veren ääni huutaa minun tyköni maasta.
verses.king_james: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother’s blood crieth unto me from the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc igitur maledictus eris super terram, quæ aperuit os suum, et suscepit sanguinem fratris tui de manu tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֖ה אָר֣וּר אָ֑תָּה מִן־הָֽאֲדָמָה֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר פָּצְתָ֣ה אֶת־פִּ֔יהָ לָקַ֛חַת אֶת־דְּמֵ֥י אָחִ֖יךָ מִיָּדֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now therefore cursed shalt thou be upon the earth, which hath opened her mouth and received the blood of thy brother at thy hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja nyt kirottu ole sinä: (kirottu) maan päältä, joka avasi suunsa ottamaan veljes verta sinun kädestäs.
verses.king_james: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother’s blood from thy hand;

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum operatus fueris eam, non dabit tibi fructus suos : vagus et profugus eris super terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י תַֽעֲבֹד֙ אֶת־הָ֣אֲדָמָ֔ה לֹֽא־תֹסֵ֥ף תֵּת־כֹּחָ֖הּ לָ֑ךְ נָ֥ע וָנָ֖ד תִּֽהְיֶ֥ה בָאָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: When thou shalt till it, it shall not yield to thee its fruit: a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be upon the earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koskas maata viljelet, niin ei pidä sen tästedes sinulle väkeänsä antaman; kulkian ja pakenevaisen pitää sinun oleman maan päällä.
verses.king_james: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Cain ad Dominum : Major est iniquitas mea, quam ut veniam merear.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר קַ֖יִן אֶל־יְהֹוָ֑ה גָּד֥וֹל עֲוֺנִ֖י מִנְּשֹֽׂא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Cain said to the Lord: My iniquity is greater than that I may deserve pardon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Kain sanoi Herralle: minun rangaistukseni on suurempi, kuin että se on kannettava.
verses.king_james: And Cain said unto the Lord, My punishment is greater than I can bear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ejicis me hodie a facie terræ, et a facie tua abscondar, et ero vagus et profugus in terra : omnis igitur qui invenerit me, occidet me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵן֩ גֵּרַ֨שְׁתָּ אֹתִ֜י הַיּ֗וֹם מֵעַל֙ פְּנֵ֣י הָֽאֲדָמָ֔ה וּמִפָּנֶ֖יךָ אֶסָּתֵ֑ר וְהָיִ֜יתִי נָ֤ע וָנָד֙ בָּאָ֔רֶץ וְהָיָ֥ה כׇל־מֹצְאִ֖י יַֽהַרְגֵֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold thou dost cast me out this day from the face of the earth, and from thy face I shall be hid, and I shall be a vagabond and a fugitive on the earth: every one therefore that findeth me, shall kill me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katsos, sinä ajat minun pois tänäpäivänä maalta, ja minun pitää sinun kasvois edestä lymymän ja tulen kulkiaksi ja pakenevaiseksi maan päällä. Ja niin minun käy, kuka ikänänsä minun löytää, se tappaa minun.
verses.king_james: Behold, thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth; and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, that every one that findeth me shall slay me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei Dominus : Nequaquam ita fiet : sed omnis qui occiderit Cain, septuplum punietur. Posuitque Dominus Cain signum, ut non interficeret eum omnis qui invenisset eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר ל֣וֹ יְהֹוָ֗ה לָכֵן֙ כׇּל־הֹרֵ֣ג קַ֔יִן שִׁבְעָתַ֖יִם יֻקָּ֑ם וַיָּ֨שֶׂם יְהֹוָ֤ה לְקַ֙יִן֙ א֔וֹת לְבִלְתִּ֥י הַכּוֹת־אֹת֖וֹ כׇּל־מֹצְאֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord said to him: No, it shall not so be: but whosoever shall kill Cain, shall be punished sevenfold. And the Lord set a mark upon Cain, that whosoever found him should not kill him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Herra sanoi hänelle: Sentähden, kuka ikänänsä Kainin tappaa, se pitää seitsemän kertaisesti kostettaman. Ja Herra pani merkin Kainiin, ettei kenkään häntä tappaisi, joka hänen löytäisi.
verses.king_james: And the Lord said unto him, Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold. And the Lord set a mark upon Cain, lest any finding him should kill him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressusque Cain a facie Domini, habitavit profugus in terra ad orientalem plagam Eden.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֥צֵא קַ֖יִן מִלִּפְנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיֵּ֥שֶׁב בְּאֶֽרֶץ־נ֖וֹד קִדְמַת־עֵֽדֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Cain went out from the face of the Lord, and dwelt as a fugitive on the earth at the east side of Eden.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin Kain läksi Herran kasvoin edestä, ja asui Nodin maalla, itään päin Edenistä.
verses.king_james: And Cain went out from the presence of the Lord, and dwelt in the land of Nod, on the east of Eden.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognovit autem Cain uxorem suam, quæ concepit, et peperit Henoch : et ædificavit civitatem, vocavitque nomen ejus ex nomine filii sui, Henoch.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֤דַע קַ֙יִן֙ אֶת־אִשְׁתּ֔וֹ וַתַּ֖הַר וַתֵּ֣לֶד אֶת־חֲנ֑וֹךְ וַֽיְהִי֙ בֹּ֣נֶה עִ֔יר וַיִּקְרָא֙ שֵׁ֣ם הָעִ֔יר כְּשֵׁ֖ם בְּנ֥וֹ חֲנֽוֹךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Cain knew his wife, and she conceived, and brought forth Henoch: and he built a city, and called the name thereof by the name of his son Henoch.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Kain tunsi emäntänsä, joka tuli raskaaksi, ja synnytti Hanokin. Ja hän rakensi kaupungin, jonka hän kutsui poikansa nimellä Hanok.
verses.king_james: And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived, and bare Enoch: and he builded a city, and called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro Henoch genuit Irad, et Irad genuit Maviaël, et Maviaël genuit Mathusaël, et Mathusaël genuit Lamech.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּוָּלֵ֤ד לַֽחֲנוֹךְ֙ אֶת־עִירָ֔ד וְעִירָ֕ד יָלַ֖ד אֶת־מְחֽוּיָאֵ֑ל וּמְחִיָּיאֵ֗ל יָלַד֙ אֶת־מְת֣וּשָׁאֵ֔ל וּמְתוּשָׁאֵ֖ל יָלַ֥ד אֶת־לָֽמֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Henoch begot Irad, and Irad begot Maviael, and Maviael begot Mathusael, and Mathusael begot Lamech,
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Hanok siitti Iradin. Irad siitti Mahujaelin. Mahujael siitti Metusaelin. Metusael siitti Lamekin.
verses.king_james: And unto Enoch was born Irad: and Irad begat Mehujael: and Mehujael begat Methusael: and Methusael begat Lamech.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui accepit duas uxores, nomen uni Ada, et nomen alteri Sella.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽקַּֽח־ל֥וֹ לֶ֖מֶךְ שְׁתֵּ֣י נָשִׁ֑ים שֵׁ֤ם הָֽאַחַת֙ עָדָ֔ה וְשֵׁ֥ם הַשֵּׁנִ֖ית צִלָּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who took two wives: the name of the one was Ada, and the name of the other Sella.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Lamek otti kaksi emäntää, yhden nimi oli Ada, ja toisen nimi Zilla.
verses.king_james: And Lamech took unto him two wives: the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Genuitque Ada Jabel, qui fuit pater habitantium in tentoriis, atque pastorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֥לֶד עָדָ֖ה אֶת־יָבָ֑ל ה֣וּא הָיָ֔ה אֲבִ֕י יֹשֵׁ֥ב אֹ֖הֶל וּמִקְנֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ada brought forth Jabel: who was the father of such as dwell in tents, and of herdsmen.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ada synnytti Jabalin, joka oli niiden isä, jotka majoissa asuivat, ja karjaa kasvattivat.
verses.king_james: And Adah bare Jabal: he was the father of such as dwell in tents, and of such as have cattle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nomen fratris ejus Jubal : ipse fuit pater canentium cithara et organo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשֵׁ֥ם אָחִ֖יו יוּבָ֑ל ה֣וּא הָיָ֔ה אֲבִ֕י כׇּל־תֹּפֵ֥שׂ כִּנּ֖וֹר וְעוּגָֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And his brother's name was Jubal: he was the father of them that play upon the harp and the organs.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hänen veljensä nimi oli Jubal, josta kantelein ja huiluin soittajat tulivat.
verses.king_james: And his brother’s name was Jubal: he was the father of all such as handle the harp and organ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sella quoque genuit Tubalcain, qui fuit malleator et faber in cuncta opera æris et ferri. Soror vero Tubalcain, Noëma.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְצִלָּ֣ה גַם־הִ֗וא יָֽלְדָה֙ אֶת־תּ֣וּבַל קַ֔יִן לֹטֵ֕שׁ כׇּל־חֹרֵ֥שׁ נְחֹ֖שֶׁת וּבַרְזֶ֑ל וַֽאֲח֥וֹת תּֽוּבַל־קַ֖יִן נַֽעֲמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Sella also brought forth Tubalcain, who was a hammerer and artificer in every work of brass and iron. And the sister of Tubalcain was Noema.
verses.finnish_biblia: Zilla myös synnytti TubalKainin, joka oli taitava kaikkinaisissa vaski-ja rautatöissä, ja Tubalkainin sisar oli Naema.
verses.king_james: And Zillah, she also bare Tubal–cain, an instructer of every artificer in brass and iron: and the sister of Tubal–cain was Naamah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Lamech uxoribus suis Adæ et Sellæ :   Audite vocem meam, uxores Lamech ;  auscultate sermonem meum :  quoniam occidi virum in vulnus meum,  et adolescentulum in livorem meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר לֶ֜מֶךְ לְנָשָׁ֗יו עָדָ֤ה וְצִלָּה֙ שְׁמַ֣עַן קוֹלִ֔י נְשֵׁ֣י לֶ֔מֶךְ הַאְזֵ֖נָּה אִמְרָתִ֑י כִּ֣י אִ֤ישׁ הָרַ֙גְתִּי֙ לְפִצְעִ֔י וְיֶ֖לֶד לְחַבֻּרָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Lamech said to his wives Ada and Sella: Hear my voice, ye wives of Lamech, hearken to my speech: for I have slain a man to the wounding of myself, and a stripling to my own bruising.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Lamek sanoi emännillensä, Adalle ja Zillalle: te Lamekin emännät, kuulkaat minun ääntäni, ja vaari ottakaat siitä kuin minä sanon: minä olen miehen tappanut minulleni haavaksi, ja nuorukaisen minulleni sinimarjaksi:
verses.king_james: And Lamech said unto his wives, Adah and Zillah, Hear my voice; ye wives of Lamech, hearken unto my speech: for I have slain a man to my wounding, and a young man to my hurt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Septuplum ultio dabitur de Cain :  de Lamech vero septuagies septies.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֥י שִׁבְעָתַ֖יִם יֻקַּם־קָ֑יִן וְלֶ֖מֶךְ שִׁבְעִ֥ים וְשִׁבְעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Sevenfold vengeance shall be taken for Cain: but for Lamech seventy times sevenfold.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kain kostetaan seitsemän kertaisesti, mutta Lamek seitsemän ja seitsemänkymmenen kertaisesti.
verses.king_james: If Cain shall be avenged sevenfold, truly Lamech seventy and sevenfold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognovit quoque adhuc Adam uxorem suam : et peperit filium, vocavitque nomen ejus Seth, dicens : Posuit mihi Deus semen aliud pro Abel, quem occidit Cain.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֨דַע אָדָ֥ם עוֹד֙ אֶת־אִשְׁתּ֔וֹ וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֔ן וַתִּקְרָ֥א אֶת־שְׁמ֖וֹ שֵׁ֑ת כִּ֣י שָֽׁת־לִ֤י אֱלֹהִים֙ זֶ֣רַע אַחֵ֔ר תַּ֣חַת הֶ֔בֶל כִּ֥י הֲרָג֖וֹ קָֽיִן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Adam also knew his wife again: and she brought forth a son, and called his name Seth, saying: God hath given me another seed for Abel, whom Cain slew.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Adam taas tunsi emäntänsä, ja hän synnytti pojan, ja kutsui hänen nimensä Set, sanoen: Jumala on minulle toisen siemenen antanut Habelin edestä, jonka Kain tappoi.
verses.king_james: And Adam knew his wife again; and she bare a son, and called his name Seth: For God, said she, hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et Seth natus est filius, quem vocavit Enos : iste cœpit invocare nomen Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּלְשֵׁ֤ת גַּם־הוּא֙ יֻלַּד־בֵּ֔ן וַיִּקְרָ֥א אֶת־שְׁמ֖וֹ אֱנ֑וֹשׁ אָ֣ז הוּחַ֔ל לִקְרֹ֖א בְּשֵׁ֥ם יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: But to Seth also was born a son, whom he called Enos: this man began to call upon the name of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Set myös siitti pojan, ja kutsui hänen Enos. Silloin ruvettiin saarnaamaan Herran nimestä.
verses.king_james: And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enos: then began men to call upon the name of the Lord.
